text.title: vui-testing-best-practices Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the vui-testing-best-practices methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Test plan MUST declare four layers — unit, integration, user, stress — with owner and cadence per layer. Skipping any layer requires documented justification.
rule.rationale: Each layer catches different failure mode; skipping leaves blind spots.

rule.statement: Accuracy MUST be reported per subgroup (accent, age, gender). Overall accuracy without subgroup breakdown is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Subgroup gaps of 15-30 pp invisible in overall accuracy.

rule.statement: Test MUST track completion_at_attempt_N. N=1 is success; N=2 acceptable; N≥4 is failure. Pure binary success/fail is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Many flows 'eventually complete' after backtracking; tracking attempt count exposes UX friction.

rule.statement: User layer MUST include ≥5 moderated real users. Less than 5 produces unstable signal.
rule.rationale: 5-user heuristic is established; below 5 noise dominates.

rule.statement: Stress layer MUST include ≥3 conditions chosen from {kitchen-noise, traffic-noise, multi-speaker, accented-speech, mid-utterance-interrupt}. Quiet-only stress test is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Real environments are noisy; lab-quiet results misrepresent.

rule.statement: When LLM-voice agent is used, seeds + prompts MUST be locked for regression runs. Free-floating seeds invalidate before/after comparisons.
rule.rationale: Stochastic outputs make regression detection impossible without seed locking.
